commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a WhatsApp `WebInboundMessage.admission` envelope for accepted inbound callbacks, wires it through access control and the web inbox monitor, keeps deprecated top-level aliases, and records the compatibility window in docs and the compatibility registry.

PR surface: Source +292, Tests +294, Docs +20. Total +606 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is an additive WhatsApp callback API foundation rather than a bug report with a current-main failure path. The changed behavior is source-checkable and covered by focused mocked monitor/access-control tests, with no live WhatsApp device proof claimed.
commands.review_followup: - Manual review is the right lane because the PR is maintainer-labeled and the remaining blocker is public API/deprecation acceptance, not an automatable patch defect.

target.title: refactor(whatsapp): add inbound admission foundation
target.body:
## Summary

- add a public-safe WhatsApp inbound `admission` envelope for accepted `WebInboundMessage` callbacks
- build admission from the resolved ingress/access-control result and attach it in the real web inbox monitor path
- keep blocked access-control results admission-free while preserving the existing legacy access fields
- deprecate the superseded top-level admission fields (`from`, `conversationId`, `accountId`, `accessControlPassed`, `chatType`) as live aliases over `msg.admission`
- record the admission-field deprecation in the plugin compatibility registry and docs with the same `removeAfter` window as the prior inbound-context compatibility work

## Scope

This PR is an admission foundation plus compatibility bookkeeping. It does not migrate downstream identity/peer resolution, reply delivery, agent dispatch, or post-admission lifecycle handoff consumers to read `msg.admission` yet.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/compat/registry.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/message-aliases.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/plugins/compatibility.md extensions/whatsapp/api.ts extensions/whatsapp/runtime-api.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound-policy.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/admission.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/group-conversation.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/message-aliases.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/message-aliases.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/types.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.streams-inbound-messages.test-support.ts src/plugins/compat/registry.test.ts src/plugins/compat/registry.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm docs:list`

## Notes

- Accepted WhatsApp inbound access-control results now carry `admission`; blocked results intentionally do not.
- Admission contains redacted ingress/access projections, account facts, conversation facts, and sender facts without exposing raw allowlist material.
- Deprecated top-level admission fields remain available as getter/setter aliases during the compatibility window, so old and new callback code do not drift when either side is read or updated.
- `accessControlPassed` remains derived from `admission.ingress.decision === "allow"`; writing the legacy boolean does not fabricate a partial ingress graph.
- No live WhatsApp device/Testbox pass is claimed for this PR.
target.author: mcaxtr
